enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: The figure sits on a flat stone, motionless as the dead trees around him. His robes are the color of the water. Only his eyes move when you approach — sharp, unhurried, and far too awake for a place like this.

hello.speaker: hermit
hello.text: You came from the west. Through Ashenvale, past the king's corpse. I felt the crown break from here. The marsh shuddered. Everything did.

choices.text: I found this page in the marsh. Can you read it?
I carry an iron token. It reacts to this place.
What lies beyond the eastern mist?

place.speaker: hermit
place.text: Ashenmere. Old name for old water. The bog swallowed a village once — you can still see the rooftops when the water is low and the light is wrong. Things live down there. Patient things.

east.speaker: hermit
east.text: Beyond the mist? Something older than the Hollow King. He was a symptom. The sickness runs deeper — into stone, into root. You will see, when the path opens. It is not ready for you yet. Or you for it.

why.speaker: hermit
why.text: Someone must watch the water. The things beneath it do not sleep, but they are slow, and a careful man can keep them slow. I have been careful for a very long time. It is not a life I would recommend.

leave.speaker: hermit
leave.text: Walk softly. The marsh remembers footsteps.

token-react.speaker: narrator
token-react.text: The Hermit's eyes drop to the iron in your hand. Something shifts in his expression — not surprise, but recognition. The kind that comes with a long wait ending.

token-react-2.speaker: hermit
token-react-2.text: That mark. I have seen it once before — on a page in a scholar's hand. An Elf. He came through here weeks ago, asking about the water and what lay beneath it. I told him what I tell everyone: leave it alone.

token-react-3.speaker: hermit
token-react-3.text: He did not listen. They never do. He walked east, past the dead trees, toward the dry islands. If he left a trace, it will be there. The marsh takes most things, but paper floats for a while.

token-react-accept.speaker: hermit
token-react-accept.text: Find what he left behind. Bring it here. I can read Elvish, after a fashion. If the scholar wrote down what he found, we will both wish he had not.

journal-turnin.speaker: narrator
journal-turnin.text: You hand the torn page to the Hermit. He holds it at arm's length, tilting it toward the grey light. His lips move as he reads. When he finishes, he folds the page carefully and does not give it back.

journal-read.speaker: hermit
journal-read.text: He found the entrance. Of course he did. The scholar writes that the water receded near the southern island — steps carved into the stone, going down. He calls it the Drowned Sanctum. Old name. Older than the marsh.

journal-read-2.speaker: hermit
journal-read-2.text: The last line is unfinished. He stopped writing mid-word. That tells you how quickly things change down there.

sanctum-offer.speaker: hermit
sanctum-offer.text: If you mean to follow him — and I see that you do — look for the submerged stairway on the far island. The water is low enough now. It was not before. Something opened the way.

sanctum-accept.speaker: hermit
sanctum-accept.text: Whatever you find below, do not touch the altar. Do not read the inscriptions aloud. And if something speaks to you from the dark — do not answer.
